title: Disposable absorbent articles
abstract: An array of disposable absorbent articles is described herein. The array has a first plurality of absorbent articles and a second plurality of absorbent articles. Each of the first plurality and second plurality of absorbent articles have absorbent cores having patterned end edges each having a plurality of inflection points.
claims:
Claims CLAIMS  What is claimed is: 1. An array of disposable absorbent articles, the array comprising: a first plurality of absorbent articles and a second plurality of absorbent articles, each of the first plurality of absorbent articles and the second plurality of absorbent articles comprising a topsheet; a backsheet; and an absorbent core disposed between the topsheet and the backsheet, wherein, each absorbent core has a first longitudinal side edge (388) and a second longitudinal side edge (384), a first end edge (394) connecting the first longitudinal side edge and the second longitudinal side edge on one end of the absorbent core, a second end edge (398) connecting the first longitudinal side edge and the second longitudinal side edge on an opposing second end of the absorbent core, wherein each of the first end edge and the second end edge comprise a first patterned portion (394A), a second patterned portion (394B), and a connecting portion (394C), wherein each of the first patterned portion and second pattern portion comprise a plurality of inflection points as determined via the k-curvature analysis test method, wherein a first portion of absorbent cores is associated with the first plurality of absorbent articles and wherein each of the absorbent cores of the first portion has a first length and a second portion of absorbent cores is associated with the second plurality of absorbent articles and wherein each of the absorbent cores of the second portion has a second length, and wherein the first length and second length are different. 2. The array of absorbent articles of claim 1, wherein first patterned portion and second patterned portion of the first end edge comprises a first number of inflection points, wherein the first patterned portion and second patterned portion of the second end edge comprises a second number of inflection points, wherein the first number and the second number are equal. 3. The array of absorbent articles of claim 1, wherein first patterned portion and second patterned portion of the first end edge comprises a first number of inflection points, wherein the first patterned portion and second patterned portion of the second end edge comprises a second number of inflection points, wherein the first number is greater than the second number. 4. The array of absorbent articles of claim 1, wherein first patterned portion and second patterned portion of the first end edge comprises a first number of inflection points, wherein 

 the first patterned portion and second patterned portion of the second end edge comprises a second number of inflection points, wherein the first number is less than the second number. 5. The array of absorbent articles of any of the preceding claims, wherein each of the absorbent cores comprises a first pattern distance corresponding to the first end edge and a second pattern distance corresponding to the second end edge, wherein the first pattern distance is equal to the second pattern distance. 6. The array of absorbent articles of any of claims 1-4, wherein each of the absorbent cores comprises a first pattern distance corresponding to the first end edge and a second pattern distance corresponding to the second end edge, wherein the first pattern distance is greater than the second pattern distance. 7. The array of absorbent articles of any of claims 1-4, wherein each of the absorbent cores comprises a first pattern distance corresponding to the first end edge and a second pattern distance corresponding to the second end edge, wherein the first pattern distance is less than the second pattern distance. 8. The array of absorbent articles of any of the preceding claims, wherein the first end width is between 50 percent to 90 percent of the second end width, more preferably between 63 percent to 83 percent, or most preferably between 70 percent to 75 percent. 9. The array of absorbent articles of any of the preceding claims, wherein each of the absorbent cores have a first width associated with the first end of the absorbent core and a second width associated with the second end of the absorbent core, wherein the first width is less than the second width. 10. The array of absorbent articles of claim 9, wherein the second end width is about 76 mm. 11. The array of absorbent articles of any claims 9-10, wherein the first end width is about 56 mm. 12. The array of absorbent articles of any of the preceding claims, wherein the first plurality of absorbent articles is disposed in a first package and designated as a first size and the second plurality of absorbent articles is disposed in a second package and designated as a second size, and wherein the first size and the second size are the same.
